channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic Party was fracturing Friday as a torrent of frustration and anger was unleashed at Senate Democrats, led by Sen.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MATT BROWN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent passengers fleeing onto a wing in a fraught evacuation amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN, COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: Louisiana’s first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned after a federal appeals court overturned a lower judge’s ruling Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 4 arrested in shooting death of 7-year-old Florida boy
item.description: "This is just the first round of arrests we will make in bringing Breon’s family the justice they deserve," the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Big March storm system threatens US with tornadoes, blizzards and wildfire risk
item.description: A huge storm system crossing the U.S. threatens to unleash tornadoes Friday in the Mississippi Valley, blizzards in the northern Plains and dry, gusty conditions in Texas and Oklahoma that pose a high risk of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma brought by the Biden administration’s Department of Justice, which challenged the states' immigration laws making it a crime for someone to be in the state if they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, Fox 5

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What's Pi Day all about? Math, science, pies and more
item.description: Math enthusiasts around the world, from college students to rocket scientists, celebrate Pi Day, which is March 14 or 3/14 — the first three digits of an infinite number with many practical uses.
item.dc:creator: Curt Anderson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These breakfast chains have raised prices the most, study finds
item.description: No, it's not just Americans at home being hit hard by rising egg prices. Your favorite breakfast spots are as well. A study from FinanceBuzz examined which fast-casual restaurants have raised prices the most over the last five years.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Infant safe; 2 arrested as SFPD looking for 3rd suspect
item.description: UPDATED 2 p.m.: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (KELO) — The child that was kidnapped Thursday evening in Sioux Falls was found unharmed, police said in a news release shortly before 2 p.m. Friday. The Sioux Falls Police Department announced officers arrested two people in connection with the kidnapping.
item.dc:creator: Collete Stellinga, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department investigating dozens of universities, including UNLV, after DEI warning letter
item.description: The Education Department announced Friday it is investigating more than 50 colleges and universities over what it called "racial preferences" in academics or scholarships, a move that comes amid the Trump administration's wider crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson dies at age 93
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: This week, Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells. The recalled glass water bottles are being recalled over concerns they can crack, causing risk of cuts or slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: University of Central Florida suspends 2 fraternities for alleged hazing, including hitting pledges with cars
item.description: An investigation is underway after two fraternities at the University of Central Florida, Sigma Chi and Delta Upsilon, were suspended over hazing allegations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jewish protesters flood Trump Tower's lobby to demand Mahmoud Khalil's release
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Demonstrators from a Jewish group filled the lobby of Trump Tower on Thursday to denounce the immigration arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a pro-Palestinian activist who helped lead protests against Israel at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chili’s celebrates 50th anniversary with margarita deals, limited-edition merch
item.description: Chili’s is marking its 50th anniversary with a celebration centered around its signature margaritas. Throughout March, the restaurant will offer special deals and exclusive merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: This common mixup is causing people to accidentally put glue in their eyes
item.description: Two very similar bottles stored in the same place in the home have led to a growing number of people seeking medical attention for accidentally putting glue in their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at SC mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested in Utah nursing home may not be extradited for 1977 Hawaii murder
item.description: A man who was arrested at a Utah nursing home in connection to a 1970s Hawaii murder may be released, according to a proposed court motion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4
